explanation: Bypasses the rule by substituting the specific '/c ' flag with the equivalent '-Command' flag. Additionally, it uses the built-in '$env:APPDATA' variable to reference the Roaming directory, which prevents the literal '\AppData\' string from appearing in the process command line, evading both conditions in selection2.

Bypasses the rule by using '-c' instead of the strictly matched '/c ' (with a trailing space). Furthermore, it utilizes the PowerShell backtick escape character within the directory name ('AppData') to break the contiguous '\AppData\' string signature without affecting how PowerShell resolves the path.

Bypasses the rule by leveraging the '-EncodedCommand' parameter. The actual execution logic (IEX (Get-Content C:\Users\Public\AppData\Local\payload.ps1)) is Base64 encoded. The resulting command line logged by the system contains no references to '/c ', '\AppData\', or 'Local\', entirely evading selection2.
